title: Secure Password Hashing Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of hashing and salting in password security.

questions.question: What is the primary characteristic of a cryptographic hash function?
options: It can be easily reversed to recover the original input.
It's a one-way mathematical function that produces a fixed-length string of characters.
It's only for temporary data storage.
It automatically generates a random password for each user.

questions.question: What is a 'Salt' in the context of password hashing?
options: A way to encrypt the entire database.
A unique, random string added to a password before hashing to prevent rainbow table attacks.
An additional password required for multi-factor authentication.
A type of database indexing.

questions.question: Why are MD5 and SHA-1 no longer recommended for password hashing?
options: They are too slow for modern servers.
They are now considered insecure because they are too fast, making brute-force attacks easy.
They only work with older browsers.
They use too much memory.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using a 'slow' algorithm like Bcrypt?
options: It reduces the time it takes for a user to log in.
It increases the time it takes for an attacker to attempt a brute-force attack on a leaked database.
It automatically generates a frontend UI for login forms.
It's a requirement of all CSS frameworks.

questions.question: How is a 'Pepper' different from a 'Salt'?
options: A Salt is random for each user, while a Pepper is the same for the entire application and stored outside the database.
A Pepper is random for each user, while a Salt is the same for the entire application.
Only the Salt is used with a hashing algorithm.
None of the above.
